messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following subscription box service privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"BoxMonthly collects your name, address, payment details, and product preferences to curate and deliver your monthly subscription box. We use Shopify to manage orders and Klaviyo for email marketing, both under signed DPAs. Subscription data is retained while your account is active plus 2 years after cancellation. Your preferences may include dietary, lifestyle, or beauty product choices. With your consent, we share anonymised preference trends with brand partners. You have the right to access, correct, port, restrict, erase, and object to your data. Contact privacy@boxmonthly.com or our DPO at dpo@boxmonthly.com. We store data in the EU. We notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Contract performance implied for subscription fulfilment, but not explicitly stated. Consent implied for brand partner sharing but not named as a specific lawful basis.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Dietary preferences can reveal religious beliefs or health conditions. This risk is acknowledged implicitly but no explicit safeguard or separate consent for special category inference is stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Subscription fulfilment, email marketing, and brand partner trend sharing are separately stated purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is proportionate to curation and delivery purposes.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Active subscription plus 2 years post-cancellation is clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), and objection (Art. 21) all listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Shopify and Klaviyo named as processors with signed DPAs.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PARTIAL] EU storage stated. However, Shopify and Klaviyo are US companies — their EU data handling should reference specific transfer safeguards.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO at dpo@boxmonthly.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour supervisory authority notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not explicitly named for each purpose), Art. 9 (dietary preference as special category data not explicitly addressed), Art. 46 (Shopify and Klaviyo transfer safeguards not referenced)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Explicitly state Art. 6(1)(b) for fulfilment and Art. 6(1)(a) consent for brand partner sharing
- Obtain and document explicit consent for dietary/health preference data as potential Art. 9 special category data
- Reference Shopify and Klaviyo Standard Contractual Clauses as transfer safeguard
